notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: the provider resource exposes CreateDatabaseToolsConnection, GetDatabaseToolsConnection, UpdateDatabaseToolsConnection, and DeleteDatabaseToolsConnection plus ChangeDatabaseToolsConnectionCompartment, while the paired datasource oci_database_tools_database_tools_connections filters compartment_id, display_name, related_resource_identifier, runtime_identity, runtime_support, state, and type and returns database_tools_connection_collection from the SDK Items list.

Pinned provider implementation: create and update populate top-level polymorphic request bodies from the selected connection type, the provider can move compartment_id through ChangeDatabaseToolsConnectionCompartment, and provider mutation facts include broader nested update candidates than the published runtime uses directly. The reviewed generatedruntime client does not adopt provider-owned work-request waiters; it follows plain write calls with read-after-write or confirm-delete rereads instead.

Repo-authored lifecycle classification stays explicit: runtime treats CREATING as provisioning, UPDATING as updating, ACTIVE and INACTIVE as success, FAILED as terminal without requeue, and delete confirmation follows DELETING to DELETED or NotFound.

Repo-authored bind-versus-create and list-lookup semantics stay explicit: pre-create lookup sends compartmentId, displayName, and optional relatedResourceIdentifier to ListDatabaseToolsConnections, then narrows candidates in memory with type-aware fields including type, connectionString or url, relatedResource.identifier, userName, privateEndpointId, and runtimeSupport before deciding whether create is required.

Repo-authored mutation policy stays explicit: only advancedProperties, connectionString, definedTags, displayName, freeformTags, keyStores, privateEndpointId, proxyClient, relatedResource, url, userName, and userPassword reconcile in place. compartmentId, runtimeSupport, and type remain replacement-only drift, and post-create locks drift stays an explicit gap until the runtime rejects or reconciles it.

Repo-authored status projection and secret behavior stay explicit: the generated runtime stamps OSOK lifecycle and async conditions plus the published status read-model fields from OCI responses, and the row has no Kubernetes secret side effects.
